untranslatable_reason: Signal requires real-time dividend calendar data, option chain ITM depth analysis (calls vs puts), early exercise probability modeling, and multi-leg position state tracking. These require external data feeds and position-level logic not available in price/volume indicators. Specifically: (1) Ex-dividend date imminent detection needs corporate action calendar, (2) ITM-to-dividend ratio comparison requires options data, (3) Assignment probability on specific strikes requires Greeks/option pricing, (4) Multi-leg position tracking requires portfolio state. Proxy conditions using IV and volatility percentiles are provided but cannot capture the core early exercise/assignment asymmetry logic.
translation_notes: This is a sophisticated options spread management signal (80/85 call vertical + reverse conversion context) requiring options Greeks, dividend calendar, and multi-leg position monitoring. The entry proxy uses low historical volatility (hvol_20 < 25%) as a stand-in for 'low uncertainty about exercise' and VIX as a secondary IV proxy. Exit uses price weakness detection. However, the true signal logic—managing asymmetric profit capture across reverse conversion legs based on early exercise probability—cannot be adequately captured by price/volume indicators alone and requires options-specific data unavailable in the permitted indicator set.
